---
description: Provides a description of where DEFRA standards have been overriden and why
applyTo: '**/*.js'
---

# Local Standards Overrides (Service-Specific)

This file documents where this service intentionally deviates from the baseline standards defined in `node-backend.instructions.md`. Each row describes the area affected, the override applied, and the reason it is permitted.

| Area                      | Baseline expectation                     | Override applied                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 | Reason                                                                                                                |
| ------------------------- | ---------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| Service shape             | Full-stack or UI-capable application     | API/backend parsing service only — no server-rendered UI in scope for core flows                                                                                                                                                                                 | This service is machine-to-machine and does not render user-facing pages                                              |
| Logging implementation    | General-purpose logger                   | Use pino for structured JSON logging via project helpers under `src/common/helpers/logging/`; avoid logging full sensitive payloads                                                                                                                              | Specified by the CDP platform this application is built on                                                            |
| Testing framework         | Jest or other common test runner         | Use Vitest for all unit and integration tests                                                                                                                                                                                                                    | Specified by CDP                                                                                                      |
| Route testing             | Supertest or similar HTTP test client    | Use Hapi `server.inject()` (or project-standard route test helpers)                                                                                                                                                                                              | Specified by CDP                                                                                                      |
| Route exposure            | All routes available in all environments | Keep test/development routes environment-gated; production exposes only core operational routes (`/health`, `/connectivity-check`, `/process-packing-list`)                                                                                                      | Minimises production attack surface; `perf-test` and `ext-test` are not considered test environments for this purpose |
| Parser naming & structure | No specific convention mandated          | Keep parser/matcher/model naming aligned with existing `src/services/parsers/`, `src/services/matchers/`, and `src/services/model-headers/` structures (for example `model1.js`, `TESCO3`); see `parsers-matchers-model-headers.instructions.md` for full detail | Preserves parser discoverability and prevents model drift                                                             |
